reason: Req 1: the opening loop was instant. A single-ingredient common potion now brews in 5s on the starter cauldron, so the early game is deliberate and clicking the cauldron to speed a brew is meaningful. Applied to BOTH runs as the new baseline.

new_value: Hollow 5s round-trip · Riftscar 1800s (30 min), geometric in between
reason: Req 3: the deepest node was 80s away — trivial. Round-trip gather time now follows a geometric curve from 5s at the Hollow to 30 min at the Riftscar, so reaching the rarest ingredients is a real commitment and worker gather-speed upgrades matter enormously. Applied to both runs.

Req 2: hand-authored base topped up to 100 by a deterministic generator. Tiers escalate early=simple basic attrs, mid=elemental, late=esoteric cosmic + high volatility, with base_value / attribute magnitude / volatility scaling by tier. Applied to both runs.

Req 2: 10 hand-authored landmarks kept as named nodes; 20 procedural nodes fill the depth curve. The starting Hollow stays a clean 3-ingredient node; deeper nodes drop progressively more ingredients (up to ~10), mostly the cheaper ones of their tier. Applied to both runs.

Req 4: in 24h every strategy hit worker/machine level ~22 and bought ~190 upgrades — the upgrade treadmill was over in a day. A steeper XP curve stretches leveling so the late levels (and the tokens that fund upgrades) take weeks, not hours. After: 24h max level dropped ~22 -> ~19.

Req 4: upgrades were buyable almost as fast as tokens arrived (~17 of a single upgrade per worker in one day). A steeper cost curve makes each successive upgrade a real coin sink, so spending keeps pace with — not ahead of — the stretched leveling. cost_base stays 25 so the FIRST upgrade is still a cheap early-game reward.

Req 4: the retrieval upgrade was the single biggest cause of the 'ingredient graveyard' — at +1 flat it compounded to ~17 items per trip per worker, so gathering buried the cauldrons in ingredients they could never brew (Completionist graveyard hit 1.9M). Halving the step makes retrieval a steady long-term investment. After: Completionist graveyard 1.9M -> 0.91M, Quest Hunter 1.0M -> 0.56M.

Req 4 pacing: the first factory expansion arrived in under 2 hours. Nudged up so machine #2 lands ~1.5-3h in, keeping the machine ladder (#3 ~8-12h, #4 days, #5 a week+) as the spine of long-term progression. The deeper machine costs (75k / 600k / 3M) already gate end-game to weeks.

The req-4 curve tuning didn't just stretch pacing — slower leveling means fewer auto-clicker upgrades for the run-away Industrialist and a far healthier floor for the Quest Hunter (3.6k -> 32k coins, 25.75 -> 28.75 quests), so all four playstyles stay viable across the longer arc.
